title: Main Menu Chaos Fixing
assignees: Alina Neamtu, Clayton Coombs, Mubeen Dildar-Mia
description:
https://zayedlawoffices.com/ currently has the following main menu:

About Us, with drop down:
> About Out Injury Law Firm
> Our Attorneys
> In the News
> Personal Injury Blog
> Personal Injury Resources
> Newsroom
> Attorney Referrals
> Contingency Fee
> Employment Opportunities
> Video Center

Personal Injury, with drop down:
> Birth injuries
> Car accidents
> Medical malpractice
> Motorcycle accidents
> Nursing home abuse
> Pedestrian accidents
> Truck accidents
> Wrongful death
> view all

Illinois, with drop down:
> Chicago
> Joliet
> Aurora
> Peoria
> Rockford
> Wheaton
> view all

Florida, with drop down:
> Miami
> view all

Results, with drop down
> Case results
> Client reviews

Contact us
____________________________________________________________
There are several issues that really bother me.
TL;DR: duplicate pathways, hidden clickable parent pages, overlapping content hubs, informational clutter inside the primary navigation.

1) Personal Injury links to /chicago-personal-injury-lawyers/, but it's not clickable, and that URL is already used under Illinois
Solution: replace "Personal Injury" with "Case types" and create separate case type page for Personal Injury not focused on Chicago (double check if it doesn't already exist).

2) All elements in the Personal injury drop down link to Chicago pages
Solution: create separate case type pages not focused on Chicago (double check if it doesn't already exist).

3) Illinois and Florida link to /illinois-personal-injury-lawyer/, respectively /florida-personal-injury-lawyer/, but they are not clickable.
"View all" under Illinois and Florida go to /areas-served-in-illinois/, respectively /areas-served-in-florida/. That’s unnecessary duplication.
Solution: only keep /illinois-personal-injury-lawyer/ & /florida-personal-injury-lawyer/. Copy relevant content from /areas-served-in-illinois/ and /areas-served-in-florida/ to these pages, then 301 to them.

4) "Employment Opportunities" does not belong under "About Us" in a PI conversion funnel.
Solution: move under Contact (or even footer-only?)

5) Personal Injury Blog and Personal Injury Resources have more or less the same content which created near-duplicate pages.
Solution: content pruning and only keeping the blog; move it far right since we should prioritize case types, locations, and results.

6) Video Center doesn't bring any value. It contains:
"About" video (can be added directly to the About Us page)
"Testimonials" video (can be added to Client Reviews)
"Practice Areas" videos (can be added to their respective pages)
> remove Video Center from menu and 301 to homepage

7) In the News doesn't bring any value as a stand alone page. It contains links to 15 publications and many of the articles are old (even from 12 years ago).
Solution: integrate them into the About Us page, as media logos.

8) Newsroom doesn't serve any purpose, it's just a redundant content hub with blog posts.
Solution: completely remove it from the menu and 301 to blog.

title: Pre-Contract The Big 5 Questions
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Quickly confirm a new client controls the 5 foundations of their web presence and decide which follow up SOPs to run: Registrar, DNS, CMS, Licensing, Hosting.

Note: This should be determined during the sales process.

RACI 

  • Responsible - AM
  • Accountable - CTO
  • Consulted - IT or Dev, SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Scope

Triggers these SOPs when needed: 
  • Registrar Migration
  • DNS Access
  • DNS Migration to Cloudflare
  • Hosting or Hosting Migration
  • Website Rebuild
  • Written Content Replacement
  • Image Replacement

Procedure

  1. Registrar
    1. If client does not clearly own the domain or cannot access registrar
      1. Severity - CRITICAL
      2. Trigger - Registrar Migration SOP
  2. 3) DNS
    1. If DNS is not under client control or cannot be delegated
      1. Severity - CRITICAL if it blocks changes
      2. Trigger - DNS Access SOP
    2. If DNS must be standardized on Cloudflare
      1. Trigger - DNS Migration to Cloudflare SOP
  3. 4) CMS
    1. If not WordPress or app ownership is unclear
      1. Severity - WARNING
      2. Discuss future - Website Rebuild SOP
  4. 5) Licensing - content and media
    1. Written content not owned or unclear
      1. Severity - CRITICAL
      2. Trigger - Written Content Replacement SOP
    2. Images or video not owned or unclear
      1. Severity - CRITICAL or WARNING
      2. Trigger - Image Replacement SOP
  5. 6) Hosting
    1. If the client lacks host access or host is not acceptable
      1. Severity - WARNING or CRITICAL
      2. Trigger - Hosting or Hosting Migration SOP
    2. If host and access are fine, record as INFO


Basecamp to do list

AM
  • Pull onboarding form and review Big 5 answers
  • Decide status per item - OK, Needs SOP, Unknown
  • Open tasks for required SOPs and link them
  • Post a Big 5 summary comment with severities and blockers
IT or Dev
  • Review Registrar, DNS, Hosting findings
  • Confirm technical SOPs and note any urgent risks
SEO
  • Review CMS and Licensing findings
  • Flag content or media issues that affect SEO work
CTO or Project lead
  • Confirm all needed SOP tasks exist and are prioritized
  • Decide if any CRITICAL items must be fixed before work begins

Extra

Here is a google form that includes questions that can be incorporated into the onboarding form.
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc4KnbTlO4Y0MKirg8Ag30BPS5LOH3002b5dRfThegKB1GQDg/viewform

title: Registrar Migration SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Move a client’s domain into the client’s registrar account using delegated access where possible. No DNS or nameserver changes in this SOP.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - Client stakeholder, current domain holder
  • Informed - Project lead

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Primary domain, plus any parked or alias domains
  • Client’s chosen registrar and target account email
  • Preferred transfer window
IT logs in Basecamp
  • Current registrar and account owner contact if known
  • Current DNS host and nameservers for reference only
  • Business critical services that depend on DNS
  • Zone records export availability and method

Procedure


1) Access to destination registrar

Preferred - delegated access If destination is GoDaddy
  • AM - request delegate access for corey@clixsy.com with Domains and Products permissions
  • AM - confirm client’s GoDaddy account email and Customer Number
  • IT - accept the delegate invite and verify access to the Domains dashboard
If destination is another registrar
  • AM - add corey@clixsy.com with a role that can manage domains and contacts
  • AM - confirm how 2FA will be handled. Best practice is to keep 2FA on, then either add our operator as a temporary 2FA method, or coordinate real-time for codes
Fallback - temporary shared credentials

Basecamp to-do list


AM - Access setup
  • Request GoDaddy delegate access for corey@clixsy.com with Domains and Products
  • If not GoDaddy, work on a case-by-case basis.
  • Confirm the transfer window.
AM - Coordinate with current holder
  • If GoDaddy to GoDaddy - share Customer Number and account email for an account change
  • If different registrars - request unlock and EPP or Auth code for transfer
IT - Execute move
  • Accept incoming GoDaddy account change, or start inter-registrar transfer with EPP or Auth code
  • Approve confirmations and record timestamps in the Basecamp task
IT - Secure the domain
  • Lock domain, enable auto renew, verify payment method
  • Set contacts to client-owned addresses
  • Verify WHOIS privacy preference
IT - Record references for DNS Migration SOP
  • Add zone record export location to the task
  • Note critical DNS-dependent services
AM - Closeout
  • Post completion note with registrar, renewal date, and what changed
  • Mark tasks complete

title: DNS Migration SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Migrate a client’s DNS to Cloudflare with zero downtime. Includes access setup, zone import, brief validation, and nameserver change at the registrar.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - Client stakeholder, previous agency if applicable
  • Informed - Project lead

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Primary domain and all additional domains or sub-brands to include
  • Contact at previous agency or current DNS host to obtain zone records
  • Maintenance window
IT logs in Basecamp
  • Current DNS host and nameservers
  • Current registrar

Access to Cloudflare


Preferred - account level delegation
  • Client adds tempclixsyreports@gmail.com to their Cloudflare account with Domain Administrator or higher
  • IT verifies access to create and manage zones
Alternate - domain level delegation
  • Client uses their Cloudflare account, adds the domain but does not change nameservers
  • Client grants tempclixsyreports@gmail.com Domain Administrator on that domain
  • IT verifies access to that domain
Fallback - coordination
  • If delegation is blocked, schedule a real-time session to perform one-time actions in the client’s account

Procedure


Migrate the domains to Cloudflare
  • AM confirms the full domain list with the client
  • AM requests a snapshot of existing zone records from the current DNS owner
  • IT creates the zone in Cloudflare and imports the provided records
  • IT performs a quick parity check on key records needed for web and email

Cutover - nameserver change

AM
  • Confirm maintenance window with client and stakeholders
  • Notify previous agency to pause DNS edits during the window
IT
  • If DNSSEC is enabled at the registrar, disable DNSSEC and remove the DS record
  • Wait for the registrar to reflect the change
  • Change nameservers at the registrar to Cloudflare’s assigned pair
  • Monitor for 15 to 60 minutes

Validation - post cutover


IT
  • Verify propagation using multiple resolvers
  • Recheck web reachability for primary hostnames
AM
  • Confirm with the client that core services are functioning, including the website and email

Basecamp to-do list


AM - Access and coordination
  • Confirm all domains to include
  • Share delegation instructions and add tempclixsyreports@gmail.com with Domain Administrator access
  • Request zone record snapshot from the current DNS owner
  • Confirm maintenance window and stakeholders
IT - Prepare Cloudflare
  • Verify delegated access
  • Create zone and import provided records
  • Perform a quick parity check for web and email records
IT - Cutover
  • If applicable, disable DNSSEC and remove DS at the registrar
  • Change nameservers to the Cloudflare pair
  • Monitor for 15 to 60 minutes
IT - Validate
  • Check propagation with multiple resolvers
  • Verify web is reachable on primary hostnames
AM - Client confirmation
  • Confirm website and email function with the client
  • Mark the task complete

title: DNS Access SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Gain reliable access to a client’s DNS for ongoing needs - hosting changes, email records, verifications, third party integrations, and troubleshooting. Prefer delegated access. If the client does not own DNS, coordinate ownership or secure access. Any DNS migration will be handled in a separate SOP.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - Client stakeholder, previous agency if applicable
  • Informed - Project lead

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Full domain list
  • Checks whether the client owns the account - if not run DNS Migration SOP
IT logs in Basecamp
  • Current nameservers and registrar

Access paths


Cloudflare DNS
  • Client delegates account or domain access to tempclixsyreports@gmail.com with Domain Administrator
  • IT verifies read and write access to the zone
GoDaddy DNS
  • Client grants delegate access to corey@clixsy.com with Domains and DNS permissions
  • IT verifies DNS management under Delegated Accounts
Other DNS providers
  • Case by case
  • Prefer delegated DNS management to a Clixsy email
  • If delegation is unavailable, use temporary shared credentials in a client approved vault
  • Keep 2FA on, coordinate codes in real time

Procedure

  • AM - Confirm DNS ownership and full domain list with the client
  • AM - Request delegated access per the correct path. If a third party controls DNS, start DNS Migration SOP
  • IT - Verify access
  • IT - Record current nameservers and registrar for reference
  • AM - Document the final access method, contacts, and credential storage location in Basecamp

Basecamp to-do list


AM
  • Confirm DNS host ownership and full domain list
  • Request delegation
    • Cloudflare - add tempclixsyreports@gmail.com as Domain Administrator
    • GoDaddy - delegate DNS management to corey@clixsy.com
    • Other providers - arrange delegated access or temporary credentials
  • If a third party controls DNS, request an intro and a zone snapshot
IT
  • Verify access
  • Note current nameservers and registrar
  • Store the zone export and access details in the client folder
AM
  • Document access method and where credentials live
  • Mark task complete

title: Written Content Replacement SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

When a new client does not legally own written content on their website, coordinate with SEO to identify that content and have it rewritten. IT involvement is minimal.

Triggers

  • Client confirms they do not own website copy, or ownership is unclear
  • Contract or prior agency confirms licensed or restricted content

Scope

  • Identify pages or sections that must be replaced
  • SEO team performs rewrite using their SOPs. Optionally follow Clixsy AI Content Generation SOP

RACI

  • Responsible - SEO
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - IT, Legal or Client stakeholder
  • Informed - Project lead

Procedure

  • AM - Confirm with client that specific content is not owned
  • AM - Open a task for SEO titled “Identify and replace non-owned content for {domain}”
  • SEO - Audit site and list URLs, sections, and modules that must be replaced
  • SEO - Rewrite content following SEO rewrite SOPs. If useful, follow the AI content SOP here:
    • 📘 SOP: Requesting AI Content - DEV TEAM
  • AM - Review replacement plan with client for approval
  • IT - After approval, replace content in CMS, publish, and verify

Basecamp to-do list


AM
  • Confirm ownership gap with client
  • Create SEO task to identify and rewrite non-owned content
  • Share AI content SOP link with SEO
  • Obtain client approval on replacement copy
SEO
  • Produce list of pages and sections to replace
  • Deliver replacement copy per SEO SOPs or AI content SOP in Google Docs
IT
  • Ensure SEO has required CMS access
  • Replace content after approval and publish
  • Verify pages render correctly and metadata remains intact
  • Move task to QA/QC

title: Hosting / Migration SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Move a client’s WordPress site to the client’s chosen host with minimal downtime. Coordinate with AM and, if needed, the previous agency. DNS cutover is handled by the separate DNS Access SOP, which is required.

Recommended hosts

  • WPEngine (other recommendations upon request)
  • Clixsy optional host on a dedicated KnownHost server

RACI

  • Responsible - IT
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - Client stakeholder, previous agency if applicable
  • Informed - Project lead

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Target host selection
  • Primary domain and any alternates to be served
  • Determine maintenance window
  • Previous agency contact if applicable
IT logs in Basecamp
  • Current host and access type
  • WordPress admin access or plan to obtain a snapshot
  • Notes on current PHP version, HTTPS certificate status, and storage needs

Access setup


WordPress
  • Preferred - request WordPress admin for export
  • Fallback - request a full site snapshot from current owner if WP access is not available
Hosting delegation
  • WPEngine - delegate keith@clixsy.com with Full access, no billing
  • KnownHost - Clixsy to provision account

Procedure


1) Source capture
  • Obtain a snapshot via admin access or from previous agency
  • Put a freeze on content changes
2) Target environment
  • Restore snapshot on hosting
3) Cutover planning
  • Coordinate A and CNAME changes from access gained with the DNS Access SOP
4) Production validation
  • Purge host cache and CDN
  • Reissue or validate SSL on the live hostname

Basecamp to-do list


AM - Coordination
  • Confirm target host choice and maintenance window
  • Obtain WordPress admin or arrange snapshot with previous agency
  • Delegate host access to keith@clixsy.com (WPEngine Full, no billing) or choose KnownHost
IT - Migration prep
  • Capture full backup or obtain snapshot
  • Create target environment
  • Restore site and configure PHP, SSL, caching
IT - Validate
  • Run staging checks for pages, forms, etc
  • Coordinate DNS cutover with AM
IT - Go live
  • After DNS cutover, verify pages, forms, SSL, and performance
  • Purge caches and confirm no mixed content
  • Record final status and timestamps
AM - Closeout
  • Confirm success with client, test forms
  • Mark tasks complete

title: Website Rebuild SOP
list_title: 1. SEO PROJECT SETUP (Legal) › TECHNICAL CONDITIONAL SETUP
description:
Purpose

Rebuild a client website on WordPress using a consistent checklist, clean staging workflow, and clear QA. This SOP includes creating the Basecamp task with a standard template, coordinating design to dev mapping, executing the build, validating content and lead paths, and launching with minimal risk.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT or Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO, Designer
  • Informed - Project lead

Basecamp task creation


Create a new Basecamp task titled:
 Website Rebuild - {Client Name} - {Domain}

Paste and fill this template in the task description:

Development Site

<https://{clientslug}.clixsysites.com/wp-admin/>

Backup Site (read only reference)

<https://{clientslug}.backup.clixsysites.com/>

Design Files

<FIGMA LINK>

Github Repo

<https://github.com/ClixsyDev/{domain}/> 

Migration Checklist

<GOOGLE SHEET LINK>

Tasks

- Clean staging db
- Phone Audit
- Form Audit

Notion

<NOTION BOARD OR DOC LINK>

Development Checklist

Client: {Client Name}
Business: {Business Name}
Domain: {domain}
Production URL: <https://www.{domain}/>

Contact: {Primary contact name and email}

Registrar: {provider if known}
DNS: {host if known}
Hosting: {provider if known}
Client IT team: {email list if applicable}

Add-On Domains: {list or N/A}

Call Tracking: {provider or N/A}

PHP: {target minor version}
GitHub: {repo URL}
Staging: https://{clientslug}.clixsysites.com/
CI/CD: {develop -> staging, master -> production}

ADA Compliance: {notes or target level}

SMTP: {configured or pending}
Forms
  Software: {CF7 or Elementor or Gravity or custom}
  Stored in Database? {Yes or No}
  Anti-Spam Strategy: {captcha or honeypot}
  Email Recipient: {list}
  Emails sent to form submitter? {Yes or No}
  3rd Party Integrations: {HubSpot or Zapier or N/A}

BCC: keith@clixsy.com

JavaScript Embeds: {point to HFCM roster once centralized}
Video Hosting Provider: {YouTube or Vimeo or N/A}

Theme
  Name: {theme}
  Child: {Yes or No}
  Up to date? {Yes or No}
  Customizations? {summary}
Chatbot: {vendor or N/A}

Plugins of note: {list}

Uptime Robot: {added or pending}
Licenses: {list premium licenses and owners}

Backup Plan: {host native or IWP offsite}
Page builder: {builder name}
Custom Post Type: {list}
Review Embeds: {Clixsy Reviews or other}

Current Cache or Optimization Strategy: {WP Rocket or host layer}
Tools > Site Health: {notes}

PSI
  desktop - {score}
  mobile - {score}
Core Vitals: {Good or Needs improvement}

Prev Agency emails: {list or N/A}
Prev Agency artifacts: {links}
Site Admin: {who}

Form & Phone Audit: {link to workbook}

Post-Development Checklist
{leave section for QA to fill}

Post-Deployment Checklist
{leave section for go-live to fill}


Procedure


1) Project setup
  • AM - Confirm scope, timelines, and design handoff in Figma.
  • IT or Dev - Run Dev Ops SOP to provision staging at {clientslug}.clixsysites.com, create repo, deploy keys, and CI or CD.
  • IT or Dev - Optional backup site mirror for reference by request.
  • IT or Dev - Create the Website Rebuild task in Basecamp.  Post all working links into the Basecamp task template.
2) Baseline and inventories
  • IT or Dev - Run System Admin SOP baseline captures on the source site
    HTTrack, wget, Screaming Frog, BuiltWith, Wappalyzer.
  • IT or Dev - Run WebDev Audit - Third Party JavaScript and centralize scripts into HFCM on staging.
  • IT or Dev - Run WebDev Audit - Phone Number and Forms to generate the workbook.
  • AM - Share any must keep tags and numbers, confirm recipients and integrations.
3) Design and SEO alignment
  • PM - Deliver the Build Specification and the template mapping into the Migration Checklist sheet.
  • SEO - Participate during design to confirm focus keywords, internal link patterns, and address client’s notes.
  • AM or Designer - Ensure image assets, attorney photos, review content, and copy updates are available.
  • Designer - Before migration, create the Figma migration map showing old templates to new templates with arrows for section to section moves. Share with IT or Dev and the content editor team.
4) Block driven build from Figma
  • IT or Dev - Break the Figma design into a Notion board.
  • IT or Dev - Build templates using Gutenberg block patterns.
    • Create reusable blocks for hero, grids, FAQs, cards, review sliders, attorney cards, location cards, CTAs.
    • Centralize embeds in HFCM. Replace theme hardcodes where feasible.
  • IT or Dev - Implement reviews module or shortcodes as specified.
  • IT or Dev - Configure forms.
    • Standardize fields and anti spam.
    • Thank You page pattern only after AM approval.
    • Set recipients and test with MailHog on staging.
  • IT or Dev - Ensure Schedule Engine or other booking widgets are included if present on the old site.
  • IT or Dev - Add phone number in header if required by design or AM.
  • IT or Dev - Add pagination for blogs per design. Avoid infinite scroll unless approved.
5) SEO migration planning and content migration
  • SEO - Complete the Migration Checklist for every crawled URL. Choose one: keep page, merge and 301, change URL and 301, or sunset. Provide target slugs and titles where relevant.
  • IT or Dev - Migrate priority pages first: Home, Attorneys or Services, Locations, Reviews, About, Contact.
  • Content editors - Use the Figma migration map to move section content correctly template to template.
  • PM - Track exceptions with red flags for out of scope or pending copy.
  • SEO - Validate meta, schema needs, and internal links.
6) QA and snagging
  • PM or Designer - Use PH to log issues on key pages.
  • IT or Dev - Resolve PH notes and reply in Basecamp.
  • IT or Dev - Re run Screaming Frog for 404s, title or meta or H1 checks, and phone or form extractions.
  • IT or Dev - Validate GTM tags.
  • IT or Dev - Verify PSI on staging for desktop and mobile. Record scores.
  • AM - Give approval for snagging and pass to client.
7) Client review
  • PM - Announce Review or QA phase and list pages ready.
  • AM - Collect consolidated feedback. Avoid piecemeal changes where possible.
  • IT or Dev - Address client changes. Update the Migration Checklist and PH comments.
8) Pre launch checks
  • IT or Dev - Confirm prior SOPs are green or queued
    • DNS Access in place, Hosting target confirmed, SSL plan, SMTP plan.
    • Forms recipients verified, test submissions pass, Thank You pages approved if used.
    • HFCM roster final, GTM reconciled, consent posture documented.
  • IT or Dev - Reconfirm no 404s, menus match production intent, structured data if applicable.
  • SEO - Final approval to proceed to production.
  • AM - Final approval to proceed to production.
9) Launch
  • IT or Dev - Migrate to target host per Hosting or Migration SOP.
  • IT or Dev - Run post deployment checklist
     SSL issuance, cache purge, asset 200s, console errors, forms end to end test to inbox, phone link taps, redirects live.
  • AM - Notify client, test forms, open observation window, and schedule post launch check in.
10) Post launch
  • IT or Dev - Run Core Vitals SOP baseline on production and attach screenshots.
  • IT or Dev - Re run Screaming Frog limited crawl for regressions.
  • SEO - Verify indexable templates, canonicals, and priority pages in GSC.
  • AM - Gather approvals and close out. Funnel remaining items into maintenance or enhancement tasks.

Basecamp to do list


AM
  • Gather design links, image libraries, review policy, and migration priorities.
  • Confirm must keep scripts, phone numbers, form recipients, and booking widgets.
  • Coordinate client approvals and final launch timing.
PM or Designer
  • Deliver template mapping and call out special templates, for example case types vs blog.
  • Provide missing templates like search results or 404 if needed.
  • Produce the Figma migration map showing old to new section mapping.
  • Review staging and leave PH comments.
IT or Dev
  • Create the Website Rebuild task with the template above.
  • Provision staging and repo per Dev Ops SOP.
  • Complete System Admin baseline captures of source site.
  • Run Third Party JS, Phone and Forms, and GTM audits and integrate findings.
  • Create Notion tasks per template and per section.
  • Build Gutenberg blocks from Figma, migrate content, centralize scripts in HFCM.
  • Configure forms, SMTP plan, and Thank You pages only with AM approval.
  • Resolve 404s, verify PSI, reconcile GTM vs hardcoded tags.
  • Prepare launch and execute Hosting or Migration SOP.
SEO
  • Participate during design for keyword targeting and content modules.
  • Complete the Migration Checklist with keep or merge and 301 or change URL and 301 or sunset decisions.
  • Validate metadata and internal links.
Project lead
  • Track schedule, unblock resources, and call final readiness review.

Notes and patterns from prior rebuilds

  • Map Figma templates explicitly to URLs. Clarify exceptions early.
  • Provide search results and 404 designs when search is present.
  • Reviews modules benefit from batch content entry with a load more pattern. Avoid infinite scroll unless approved.
  • If the legacy site had Schedule Engine or similar, enable it on staging for parity.
  • Centralize all third party JS in HFCM and reconcile with GTM to avoid double firing.
  • Keep a single form pattern where possible. Document deviations in the Migration Checklist.
  • Use the Migration Checklist as the source of truth for page readiness, red flags, redirects, and go live scope.

title: Onboarding SOP
assignees: Ethan Vandenberg, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
sunset this task?  This might be for the AM's to create.

Purpose

Kick off a new client efficiently by collecting onboarding info, confirming ownership and access, then selecting the correct follow on SOPs. This SOP starts before the kickoff call.

RACI

  • Responsible - AM
  • Accountable - CTO
  • Consulted - SEO, IT
  • Informed - Project lead

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Schedule a kickoff call
  • Share the Onboarding Form and request completion
  • Ensure The Big 5 is complete
IT logs in Basecamp
  • Note any risks from The Big 5 answers
  • Confirm whether site ownership and preferred hosting are established

Prerequisites

  • The Big 5 completed
  • DNS access established per DNS Access SOP
  • Hosting path identified per Hosting, or Hosting Migration SOP queued if required

Procedure

  • AM - Send the Onboarding Form and set a due date
  • AM - Schedule the kickoff call and include the required attendees
  • IT and SEO - Review form responses and The Big 5 summary
  • AM - Decide which SOPs apply using the Trigger Matrix below
  • AM - Create Basecamp tasks for all selected SOPs and assign owners
  • AM - Confirm the plan with the client during the kickoff call
  • AM - Post the final onboarding plan and timelines in Basecamp

Trigger Matrix


Always run for new clients
  • Onboarding SOP
  • WebDev Audits SOPs bundle
    • Core Web Vitals and performance
    • Third party JavaScript audit, move to HFCM
    • Phone number audit
    • Form audit, emails, thank you pages, webhooks, database store
    • Hosting stack check, registrar, DNS, hosting, web server, PHP version
    • WordPress audit, plugins, theme, code review, page builders, licenses
  • DevOps SOP
  • System Admin SOP
  • Final Report SOP
Conditional
  • Hosting Migration or Access SOP - required if hosting needs to change or access is missing
  • Content Replacement SOP - required if client does not own written content
  • Image Replacement SOP - required if client does not own images
  • Website Rebuild SOP - only if site requires a rebuild
  • GTM Audit - coming soon, deep dive, include only when requested or clearly needed
Already handled upstream
  • Registrar Migration SOP - invoke if registrar is not under client control
  • DNS Access SOP - invoke if DNS access is missing
  • DNS Migration to Cloudflare SOP - only if ownership or access requires migration

Basecamp to-do list


AM
  • Share Onboarding Form and set due date
  • Schedule kickoff call and invite stakeholders
  • Confirm The Big 5 completion
  • Build the SOP plan using the Trigger Matrix
  • Create and assign Basecamp tasks for selected SOPs
  • Post the onboarding plan and timelines
IT
  • Review form and Big 5 responses for risks and access gaps
  • Confirm registrar, DNS, and hosting access status
  • Flag any blockers requiring Registrar Migration, DNS Access, or Hosting Migration
SEO
  • Review scope indicators for content and image ownership
  • Prepare for WebDev Audits bundle
AM

  • Capture decisions and next steps

Onboarding Prompt

Use ChatGPT and paste the results from the onboarding form into this prompt.  Use the results to update the downstream SOP's as necessary.

On the Google Sheets document for the Form Responses, use this formula to transpose the data and make it ready to paste into ChatGPT:

=transpose(1:2)

You are a Clixsy onboarding assistant. Turn the client’s onboarding form into a clean Basecamp task. Keep it short and scannable.
Inputs
Paste form responses below:
<<PASTE ONBOARDING FORM RESULTS HERE>>

Rules

Open questions for the client

  •  <bullet list of missing or unclear answers> 

Links

title: WebDev Audit - Core Vitals
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
Purpose

Ensure a client’s website passes Core Web Vitals. Start with the home page on desktop and mobile using Lighthouse and PageSpeed Insights benchmarks, then monitor sitewide health in Google Search Console. Technical implementation details live in internal playbooks.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT/Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • AM invokes for any new or existing client
  • New launch or major redesign
  • Performance complaints or visibility drops
  • GSC shows Needs improvement or Poor for any Core Web Vitals metric

Procedure


Baseline
  • IT/Dev - Run Lighthouse and PageSpeed Insights for the home page on desktop and mobile
  • IT/Dev - Capture screenshots of PSI and Core Vitals scores and attach to the task as the benchmark
Optimize
  • IT/Dev - Apply performance improvements using standard stack and host features
  • IT/Dev - Re test home page with Lighthouse and PSI until targets are reached or tradeoffs are documented
Validate and monitor
  • IT/Dev - Verify Core Web Vitals status in Google Search Console for all indexed pages
  • IT/Dev - Create a short list of URLs in Needs improvement or Poor and open follow up subtasks
  • IT/Dev - Optional: connect GSC to Screaming Frog and export a report mapping URLs to CWV pass or fail for delivery to AM and SEO
Observe window
  • AM - Leave the task open for up to 28 days to allow CrUX field data to reflect changes
  • AM - Update client weekly with status
  • IT/Dev - If pages remain in Needs improvement or Poor, document constraints and next actions
Close and handoff
  • IT/Dev - Post final PSI and Core Vitals screenshots for home page and a brief GSC summary
  • AM - Communicate outcomes and any remaining items to the client

Notes and expectations

  • Clixsy built sites - we guarantee pass targets under our supported stack
  • Third party or inherited sites with heavy page builders or bloat - best effort, no guarantee

Basecamp to do list


AM
  • Confirm URLs and access for WordPress and GSC
  • Open the WebDev Audit - Core Vitals SOP task and assign IT/Dev
  • Provide weekly client updates during the observation window
IT/Dev
  • Capture PSI baseline screenshots for home page - desktop and mobile
  • Optimize and re test until targets are met or constraints documented
  • Review GSC Core Web Vitals - list URLs needing attention
  • Optional - attach Screaming Frog + GSC report
  • Post final PSI screenshots and a brief GSC summary
AM
  • Share outcome with the client, including any remaining action items
  • Mark task complete

title: WebDev Audit - Third Party Javascript
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
Purpose

Inventory all third party scripts on a client site, centralize them in HFCM Header Footer Code Manager, and produce a clear report for AM and client review. Goal - know what is loaded, why it exists, and whether to keep, consolidate, or remove.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT/Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • New client onboarding
  • Site performance concerns or bloat
  • Tag governance cleanup request

Procedure


1) Discover and inventory
  • Collect all script tags, inline JS, external JS, pixels, and verification tags
  • Perform crawls and compare with WordPress code snippets
  • Note where each script is injected
    • Theme files, functions, widgets, page builder blocks, GTM, plugin settings
2) Centralize in HFCM
  • Install and activate Header Footer Code Manager (HFCM)
  • For each discovered script, create an HFCM snippet with:
    • Name, placement (Header or Footer), display rules (site wide or specific URLs), load once per page setting
    • Paste the exact code
  • Disable the original injection point safely
    • Comment out in theme or turn off plugin setting
  • Validate parity
    • View source and network requests before and after, confirm no duplicates
3) Classify and document
  • For every snippet, record:
    • What it is, why it exists, who cares, impact if removed
    • Location and scope
    • IDs or keys found, for example GTM container ID, GA4 measurement ID, Pixel ID
  • Use the ChatGPT prompt below to turn raw snippets into a client friendly summary
4) AM review and decisions
  • Send the summary to AM
  • AM confirms with client which scripts are still needed
  • IT/Dev applies decisions
    • Keep, disable, or remove
    • Consolidate duplicates, for example move GA4 from hardcoded gtag to GTM later if desired
5) Finalize and handoff
  • Update HFCM names and notes to reflect decisions
  • Post the final script roster and decisions

ChatGPT prompt for script explanations


Paste this into ChatGPT with the raw JS snippets or a list of tags.

You are auditing third party JavaScript on a WordPress site. I will paste one or more script tags, pixels, or tag manager snippets. Produce a concise, non-technical summary per item with this exact structure:

<Short name> • <Scope: Site wide or Page specific> • <Placement: Header or Footer>

What it is: 1 to 2 sentences that identify the tool and its purpose. Include vendor and any IDs you can parse, for example GTM-XXXX, GA4 G-XXXX, Ads AW-XXXX, Meta Pixel ID, Microsoft UET, verification tokens, chat widgets, accessibility widgets, scheduling bridges.
Why it exists: Plain English business reason, for example analytics, ads conversion tracking, call tracking, chat, accessibility, scheduler.
Who cares: Teams impacted, for example Marketing, Analytics, Ads, Sales, Support, Compliance.
If removed: Specific impact in one sentence.
Ask the client: A yes or no question that helps decide whether to keep it.
Rec: One of Keep, Remove, or Consolidate, with 1 short reason.

At the top, output a compact index list like:
- Google Tag Manager - container GTM-XXXXX
- Google tag - GA4 G-XXXX and Ads AW-XXXXX
- Meta Pixel - pixel ID XXXXXXXXXX
- Microsoft UET - tag ID XXXXXXXX
- [other items you detect]

Use the client friendly tone above. Do not invent IDs. If an ID is missing, write "key not present".
Now analyze these snippets:
<PASTE SCRIPTS HERE>


Basecamp to do list


AM
  • Confirm expected must keep tags
  • Share the IT summary with the client and gather decisions
  • Post final approvals
IT/Dev
  • Discover scripts across templates and pages
  • Centralize in HFCM, disable originals, validate parity
  • Generate the ChatGPT summary using the prompt and attach results
  • Apply keep or remove decisions and export HFCM config
AM
  • Share the finalized roster with the client
  • Mark task complete

title: WebDev Audit - Google Tag Manager (GTM)
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
Purpose

Identify every Google Tag Manager container on the site, gain access, export tags, triggers, and variables, then produce a client friendly report like the Third Party JavaScript audit. IT or Dev will extract and summarize. AM will review with the client and decide what to keep, consolidate, or remove.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT or Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • New client onboarding
  • Unknown or conflicting tracking
  • Tag governance cleanup request

Procedure


1) Discover GTM containers
  • Locate all occurrences of GTM container IDs in <script> and <noscript> on the site
  • Record each GTM-XXXXX found and where it appears
2) Gain access
  • AM requests access for the Clixsy operator email to the GTM account or specific containers
  • Access level: Admin preferred, Edit at minimum
3) Export container data
In Google Tag Manager:
  • Identify the live container version and export as JSON
  • Export the current workspace as JSON if changes are pending
  • Record container name, ID, environments, and any linked GA4 properties if visible
4) Summarize with ChatGPT
  • Paste the exports into ChatGPT using the prompt below to produce a client friendly summary of tags, triggers, variables, destinations, and risks
5) Finalize and handoff
  • Attach the ChatGPT summary, the raw JSON exports, and a short decision list for AM review
  • Propose actions: keep, remove, consolidate into GTM, or migrate from GTM to platform native if appropriate

ChatGPT prompt for GTM explanations


Paste this prompt with the exported GTM JSON. You can split large exports into multiple messages.

You are auditing a Google Tag Manager setup for a WordPress site. I will paste one or more GTM container exports (JSON) representing the live container version and possibly a workspace.

Produce a concise, non-technical summary that a client and AM can understand. Follow this exact structure:

Index
- Container(s): <container name> - <GTM-XXXXX> - <environment if present>
- Total tags: <count>
- Total triggers: <count>
- Total variables: <count>
- Key platforms detected: GA4, Google Ads, Microsoft UET, Meta Pixel, LinkedIn Insight, Call tracking, Chat widgets, A/B tools, other
- Potential duplicates or overlaps: list briefly

Per item summaries - group by platform
<Short name> • Scope: <Site wide or Page specific> • Fires on: <event or pages>
What it is: 1 to 2 sentences describing the tag and vendor. Include IDs you can parse, for example GA4 G-XXXX, Ads AW-XXXX, UET ID, Pixel ID.
Why it exists: Plain English reason - analytics, ads conversions, call tracking, chat, A/B testing, consent.
Who cares: Teams impacted - Marketing, Analytics, Ads, Sales, Support, Compliance.
If removed: One sentence impact.
Ask the client: A yes or no question that helps decide whether to keep it.
Rec: Keep, Remove, or Consolidate with a 1 line reason.

Triggers overview
- List each major trigger with a short description, for example All pages, Some pages - contact, Form submission - CF7, Click - phone link, Timer - 5s, Consent events.

Variables overview
- List important variables - dataLayer keys, consent mode, URL parts, form data, UTM capture, with 1 line about usage.

Risk and hygiene checklist
- Duplicates with hardcoded scripts or HFCM
- Tags firing on all pages that should be scoped
- Old Universal Analytics or unused tags
- Missing consent or reliance on blocked cookies
- Sensitive data risks - if any detected in parameters

Output requirements
- Do not invent IDs. If an ID is missing, write "id not present".
- Keep each item to 6 lines max.
- End with a short action list: Keep, Remove, Consolidate, Investigate - with counts.

Now analyze these GTM exports:
<PASTE GTM JSON HERE>


Basecamp to do list


AM
  • Gain GTM access
  • Review the summary with the client and confirm Keep or Remove or Consolidate decisions
IT or Dev
  • Discover all GTM containers on the site
  • Export live container version and workspace JSON
  • Generate the ChatGPT summary and attach all artifacts
  • Flag duplicates with the Third Party JavaScript audit and propose consolidations

title: WebDev Audit - WordPress Stack
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
Purpose

Assess a client’s WordPress build quality at the application level. Inventory plugins, theme and page builder usage, custom code, licenses, and existing security measures. Identify risks and whether a rebuild should be considered. This is a passive report for AM, SEO, and Dev. No hosting stack items here.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT/Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • New client onboarding
  • Security or stability concerns
  • Plugin bloat or unexplained features
  • Rebuild discussion

Scope

  • Plugins and licenses, including whether each plugin is actually in use
  • Theme and page builder structure and code quality
  • Custom code and snippet locations
  • Update posture inside WordPress only
  • Existing security features already present
  • Client content workflows

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Confirm WordPress admin access and any known paid licenses the client owns
  • Start a shared list of client workflows to support
    • Examples: blog posts, coupons, attorney edits, location pages
IT/Dev logs in Basecamp
  • Ensure backup and staging exist before any exploratory checks
  • Review workflow list for technical touchpoints

Procedure


1) Plugin inventory, license, and in use audit
  • Export full plugin list with version and activation state
  • For each plugin, determine if it is in use
    • Evidence options: active shortcodes, widgets, blocks, registered post types or taxonomies, scheduled actions, enqueued assets on front or admin, settings pages with configured data, code references in theme or mu-plugins
  • Capture license holder, term, and update channel for premium plugins
  • Flag duplicates or overlap in functionality
  • Tag status: In use, Not in use, Unsure
  • Tag risk: OK, Replace, Investigate, Unknown License
2) Theme, child theme, page builders, and code quality
  • Identify active theme and child theme presence
  • Record page builder and version, note mixed builder use if any
  • Review code structure for quality signals
    • Consistent templating, separation of concerns, minimal hard coded URLs, reusable components
  • Hunt for risky or fragile patterns
    • curl or remote webhooks, direct DB calls, suppressed errors, eval, base64_decode, remote includes, unescaped output, disabled updates
  • Note custom post types, shortcodes, or modules that create lock in or complexity
3) Custom code and snippet mapping
  • Locate custom code in child theme, mu-plugins, snippets plugin, functions.php, HFCM, code inserters
  • For each custom piece, record location, purpose, entry points, and any external calls or keys
4) Update posture inside WordPress
  • Record current WordPress core branch only
    • Confirm the site is on the current major branch, not the exact version
  • List pending plugin or theme updates and any blockers inside WordPress
5) Existing security and reliability features
  • Observe what already exists, do not change anything in this SOP
    • wp-login changes or hide-login plugins, 2FA plugins, audit logs, firewall plugins, reCAPTCHA or hCaptcha, rate limiting, comment or form spam controls
  • Note user role hygiene issues or orphaned admins if visible
6) Client workflows and operational fit - joint AM + IT/Dev
  • Map real workflows
    • Blog creation, coupon updates, attorney bio edits, location updates
  • For each workflow, document: trigger, steps, responsible role, tool or plugin used, bottlenecks, and required SLAs
7) Recommendations
  • Provide quick wins, medium lifts, and rebuild criteria
  • For each Not in use plugin, recommend removal after confirmation
  • For Unknown License or lapsed license, recommend a licensing plan or replacement

Site summary
  • WordPress major branch
  • Multisite status if applicable
  • Notable application level notes
Plugins
  • Columns: Name, Version, Active, In use (Yes or No or Unsure), Evidence of use, Overlap, License owner, License status, Risk (OK or Replace or Investigate or Unknown License), Recommendation
Theme and builder
  • Active theme, child theme status
  • Builder in use and version
  • Structure and code quality notes
  • Risky patterns observed
Custom code
  • Location, Purpose, External calls or webhooks, Keys present, Risk notes, Action
Updates inside WordPress

Existing security features
  • Hide login present, 2FA, WAF or firewall plugin, captcha, audit logs, role hygiene notes
Client workflows - joint AM + IT/Dev
  • Workflow, Trigger, Steps, Responsible role, Tool or plugin, Friction, Recommendation
Recommendations summary

  • Medium lifts
  • Rebuild recommendation and rationale if applicable

  • Co lead the workflow discovery session with IT/Dev
  • Share the final audit with the client and gather decisions
IT/Dev

title: System Admin SOP
description:
Purpose

Implement Clixsy standard operations on a client WordPress site for security, backups, monitoring, and maintainability. Enroll the site in routine maintenance and capture a baseline snapshot of the site and tech stack.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • New client onboarding
  • Post migration to new hosting
  • Security or monitoring gap found

Procedure


0) Baseline capture and tech fingerprint

title: Onboarding Final Report
description:
Should we also sunset this report?  This was originally created to solicit follow-up but may not be necessary or a technical task.

Purpose

Assemble a single client report that summarizes findings and actions from all onboarding SOPs. Every item is categorized by severity - INFO, WARNING, CRITICAL - and grouped by department so AMs can act quickly and consistently.

RACI

  • Responsible - AM
  • Accountable - CTO
  • Consulted - IT or Dev, SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Severity definitions

  • CRITICAL - High risk of downtime, data loss, security exposure, legal or ownership gap, or broken lead flows. Needs immediate action.
  • WARNING - Noticeable risk, performance issue, or unclear ownership that could impact results. Requires a decision from AM, possibly with client input.
  • INFO - Facts, context, and low risk notes that guide future decisions.

Inputs

Pull source material and attachments from the completed SOPs:
  • Registrar Migration
  • DNS Access and DNS Migration to Cloudflare
  • Hosting or Migration and Hosting Stack
  • Dev Ops
  • System Admin
  • WebDev Audits - Core Vitals, Third Party JavaScript, Phone Number and Forms, WordPress
  • WebDev Audit - Google Tag Manager
  • Written Content Replacement, Image Replacement
  • Onboarding plan and decisions

Procedure


Compile findings
Triage and assign severity
  • Apply the severity definitions above
  • When in doubt, mark as WARNING and note what is needed to reclassify
Organize by department
  • Place each item under AM, IT or Dev, SEO, or Project lead
Build the report
  • Use the Final Report Prompt below. Keep formatting and section order consistent
Review and publish
  • AM reviews CRITICAL items with IT or Dev and SEO, then gets client approvals where needed
  • Post the report Google Doc link in Basecamp with a short executive summary
  • Open follow up to dos for every CRITICAL and WARNING item

Final Report Prompt

Paste the outputs from every SOP below this prompt where indicated. Do not include private credentials. Follow the output format exactly.

You are a reporting assistant. Create a clear, client ready "Onboarding Final Report" from the pasted SOP outputs. Follow these rules:

GOAL
- Summarize findings and actions across all onboarding SOPs.
- Categorize every item by Severity: CRITICAL, WARNING, INFO.
- Group items by Department: AM, IT or Dev, SEO, Project lead.
- Create 7 day and 30 day action plans with owners and due dates.

INPUT
<<PASTE ALL SOP RESULTS AND LINKS HERE>>

SEVERITY GUIDANCE
- CRITICAL: downtime risk, data loss, security exposure, legal or ownership gap, broken lead flows.
- WARNING: performance risk, unclear ownership, EOL within 90 days, misconfigurations that could impact results.
- INFO: context or low risk notes that aid future decisions.

DEPARTMENTS
- AM: communication, approvals, vendor coordination, prioritization.
- IT or Dev: technical fixes, migrations, audits, CI or CD, monitoring.
- SEO: content or image ownership, CWV content implications, on page items.
- Project lead: scheduling, resourcing, cross team blockers.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Use this exact structure and headings. Use concise bullet points and tables. Do not invent facts. Link to evidence when available.

# Cover
Client: <name>
Report date: <YYYY-MM-DD>
Prepared by: <name>
Period covered: Onboarding through finalization

# Executive summary
- Totals - CRITICAL: <n>, WARNING: <n>, INFO: <n>
- Top 3 CRITICAL items - one line each with business impact
- 7 day plan - one line overview
- 30 day plan - one line overview

# Department items

## AM
Create a table with these exact columns:
Severity | Item | Impact | Owner | Due date | Status | Evidence link
<rows>

## IT or Dev
Severity | Item | Impact | Owner | Due date | Status | Evidence link
<rows>

## SEO
Severity | Item | Impact | Owner | Due date | Status | Evidence link
<rows>

## Project lead
Severity | Item | Impact | Owner | Due date | Status | Evidence link
<rows>

# SOP roll ups
Create short sub sections for traceability. Each line must contain a severity tag and a link when available.

## Registrar Migration
- <CRITICAL or WARNING or INFO> - <finding> - <evidence link>

## DNS Access and DNS Migration
- <severity> - <finding> - <evidence link>

## Hosting or Migration
- <severity> - <finding> - <evidence link>

## Hosting Stack
- <severity> - EOL or Near EOL items and next steps - <evidence link>

## Dev Ops
- <severity> - staging URL, repos, deploy keys present, pipeline status - <evidence link>

## System Admin
- <severity> - monitoring, backups, security logging, baseline captures - <evidence link>

## WebDev Audit - Core Vitals
- <severity> - PSI baseline and latest, GSC status, observation window - <evidence link>

## WebDev Audit - Third Party JavaScript
- <severity> - HFCM roster, duplicates removed or pending, unknowns - <evidence link>

## WebDev Audit - Google Tag Manager
- <severity> - containers and IDs, key tags, duplicates with hardcoded scripts, consent notes - <evidence link>

## WebDev Audit - Phone Number and Forms
- <severity> - unique numbers summary, form recipients and TY pages, DNI mapping - <evidence link>

## WebDev Audit - WordPress
- <severity> - plugin in use matrix, license gaps, code risks, workflow fit - <evidence link>

## Content and Image Replacement
- <severity> - pages or modules to replace, approvals status - <evidence link>

# Action plans

## 7 day action plan
Create a table with:
Item | Owner | Steps | Success criteria | Link to task

## 30 day action plan
Item | Owner | Steps | Success criteria | Link to task

# Appendices

## Unique numbers summary
Number | Instances | Decision | Replacement | Owner | Status | Evidence

## Plugin audit summary
Plugin | Version | Active | In use | Evidence of use | License status | Risk | Recommendation | Owner

## GTM audit summary
Container | GTM ID | Tags | Triggers | Variables | Duplicates noted | Consent posture | Recommendation | Owner

REDACTION AND QUALITY
- Remove secrets or credentials.
- Merge duplicates and reference the primary evidence link.
- If a severity is uncertain, classify as WARNING and note what evidence is needed.

AM
  • Gather links from each SOP task and draft the report
  • Triage items into CRITICAL, WARNING, INFO
  • Confirm owners and due dates with IT or Dev and SEO
  • Share with client, capture approvals, and open tasks for CRITICAL and WARNING items
IT or Dev
  • Validate technical classifications
  • Estimate effort for fixes
SEO
  • Validate ownership findings and rewrite or replacement statuses

title: WebDev Audit - Phone Number & Form Audit
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
Purpose

Establish a known state for a client’s lead paths. Inventory every phone number and form across the site, summarize where they appear, and confirm what each should do. This SOP can be invoked at onboarding or any time leads drop.

RACI

  • Responsible - IT/Dev
  • Accountable - AM
  • Consulted - SEO
  • Informed - Project lead

Triggers

  • New client onboarding
  • Reported loss of calls, form submissions, or tracking anomalies
  • Quarterly QA cycle

Scope

  • One combined audit for phone numbers and forms
  • Crawling and extraction are handled by IT using the internal Screaming Frog configuration
  • This SOP covers what AM and IT do with the results, not how to operate Screaming Frog

Pre-checks and inputs


AM logs in Basecamp
  • Confirm site URL, primary contact, and expected primary phone number
  • Confirm access to CallRail or other DNI tool if used
IT/Dev logs in Basecamp
  • Run the Screaming Frog crawl with the custom extractions and export the results
    • Use this Screaming Frog configuration: Screaming Frog Cheat Sheet - DEV TEAM
  • Prepare the audit workbook
    • Sheet 1: SF w/ JS raw crawl output
    • Sheet 2: Unique Numbers summary
    • Sheet 3: Forms Inventory summary

Procedure

Example: Cellino Law & Big Auto

1) Build the phone number summary
IT/Dev - In Google Sheets, add a Unique Numbers sheet with this formula to normalize and count unique numbers from the raw crawl:
=LET(
  data, TOCOL(ARRAYFORMULA(TRIM('SF'!D2:AJ)), 1, TRUE),
  norm, MAP(data, LAMBDA(x,
    LET(
      d, REGEXREPLACE(REGEXREPLACE(LOWER(x), "^tel:\s*", ""), "[^\d]", ""),
      n, IF(AND(LEN(d)=11, LEFT(d,1)="1"), RIGHT(d,10), d),
      IF(LEN(n)=10, n, "")
    )
  )),
  uniq, SORT(UNIQUE(FILTER(norm, norm<>""))),
  HSTACK(uniq, MAP(uniq, LAMBDA(u, COUNTIF(norm, u))))
)

2) Build the forms inventory
IT/Dev - In Google Sheets, add a Forms Inventory sheet with this formula to count the number of instances from the raw crawl:
=QUERY(
  TOCOL(SF!G2:K, 1, TRUE),
  "select Col1, count(Col1) where Col1 is not null group by Col1 label count(Col1) ''",
  0
)

Here are the columns:
  • Form Platform
  • Form ID
  • Form Name
  • Submissions
  • Instances
  • Description
  • Example Page
  • Admin Notification Email
  • Confirmation behavior (message or thank you page URL)
  • Spam Protection
  • Webhooks & Integrations
    • Name
    • Endpoints
3) AM review and client alignment
AM
  • Call each unique phone number ahead of the client meeting and log status
    • Status options: Connected to client, No answer, Voicemail, Wrong number, Disconnected
    • Note department or IVR path when applicable
  • Add a discussion block to the onboarding or review call agenda
  • During the call, work through each number and agree on an action
    • Keep as is
    • Replace with primary number
    • Remove from page or unlink
  • For each form, confirm intended recipients, thank you page, and CRM routing
4) Apply decisions
IT/Dev
  • Implement approved phone updates on the site
  • Implement approved form settings and notification updates
  • Re crawl targeted templates to confirm changes are reflected
5) DNI and call tracking mapping (if applicable)
AM
  • Confirm whether Dynamic Number Insertion is active, for example CallRail
  • If yes: gather access to CallRail and export number pools and swapping rules
IT/Dev
  • Extend the workbook with a DNI Mapping sheet tying page or UTM condition, displayed number, destination number, pool name, and priority
  • Optional: produce a simple flow chart showing swap rules for client review
    • Example: Bassett Services

Basecamp to do list


AM
  • Confirm expected primary number and CallRail usage
  • Schedule audit review with client and add phone discussion to agenda
  • Call each unique number and log status before the meeting
  • Lead the keep or replace or remove decision per number and confirm form settings with the client
IT/Dev
  • Run Screaming Frog crawl and build the workbook tabs
  • Populate Unique Numbers, Numbers by Page, Forms Inventory, and Form Details
  • Implement approved changes and perform a light recrawl for verification
AM
  • If DNI is in use, collect CallRail access and request a DNI mapping export
  • Share the final report and secure client sign off
  • Mark task complete

Workbook column suggestions


Unique Numbers
  • Number, Instances, Notes, Decision, Replacement number, Owner, Status
Numbers by Page
  • URL, Displayed number, Context snippet, Template or page type, Action needed
Forms Inventory
  • Form ID, Form name, Platform, Pages, Integrations, Notification recipients, Confirmation type and URL, Conditional logic summary, Instances, Notes, Action
DNI Mapping - if applicable
  • Condition or page rule, Number pool, Displayed number, Destination number, Priority, Notes
